entries.title: Color Token Priority
entries.description:
## Rule

All color usage MUST reference design tokens, never raw color values. The priority order is:

1. **Semantic tokens (highest priority)** -- Tokens that convey meaning and intent.
   - Examples: `text-primary`, `text-secondary`, `text-warning`, `text-error`, `bg-surface`, `bg-error`, `border-focus`, `icon-success`.
   - Use these whenever the color communicates a specific purpose.

2. **Contextual tokens** -- Tokens scoped to a component context.
   - Examples: `button-bg-primary`, `input-border-default`, `card-bg`.
   - Use when semantic tokens are too generic for a component's specific need.

3. **Primitive tokens (lowest priority)** -- Named palette references.
   - Examples: `blue-600`, `gray-100`.
   - Use only when no semantic or contextual token fits, and document the reason.

### Forbidden

The following are **never** permitted in source code:

- Raw hex values: `#3B82F6`, `#fff`, `#000`
- RGB/RGBA functions: `rgb(59, 130, 246)`, `rgba(0,0,0,0.5)`
- HSL/HSLA functions: `hsl(217, 91%, 60%)`
- Tailwind palette classes used as color decisions: `bg-blue-500`, `text-gray-700`
- CSS named colors: `red`, `white`, `black`

## Why

1. **Theming** -- Semantic tokens decouple color intent from color value, enabling dark mode, high-contrast mode, and brand theming with zero code changes.
2. **Consistency** -- When "error" always maps to the same token, the error color is guaranteed to be uniform across every component.
3. **Accessibility** -- Token-level contrast validation ensures all usages meet WCAG requirements when the palette changes.
4. **Maintainability** -- Changing a brand color updates one token definition rather than hundreds of scattered hex values.

## Assertions

- No source file may contain a literal hex color (`/#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}/`).
- No source file may contain `rgb(`, `rgba(`, `hsl(`, or `hsla(` as a color value.
- No Tailwind class matching `/bg-(red|blue|green|yellow|purple|pink|indigo|gray|slate|zinc|neutral|stone|orange|amber|emerald|teal|cyan|sky|violet|fuchsia|rose|lime)-\d+/` may appear in component code.
- Every color property in CSS/JSX must resolve to a design token reference.
- Primitive token usage must include a code comment explaining why no semantic token fits.

entries.title: Spacing Scale
entries.description:
## Rule

All spacing values (margin, padding, gap) MUST use only the following scale:

| Token   | Value |
|---------|-------|
| space-1 | 4px   |
| space-2 | 8px   |
| space-3 | 12px  |
| space-4 | 16px  |
| space-6 | 24px  |
| space-8 | 32px  |

No other spacing values are permitted. Allowed values: **4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32** (in pixels).

- Use `space-1` (4px) for tight inline spacing (icon-to-label gaps, error message offset).
- Use `space-2` (8px) for related element grouping (form fields within a group).
- Use `space-3` (12px) for medium internal padding (card content insets).
- Use `space-4` (16px) as the base unit for standard padding and gaps.
- Use `space-6` (24px) for section separation within a view.
- Use `space-8` (32px) for major section breaks and page-level margins.

## Why

A constrained spacing scale based on a 4px grid creates consistent visual rhythm across all screens and components. When every element snaps to the same spatial grid:

1. **Visual consistency** -- Users perceive the interface as polished and intentional. Arbitrary values (e.g., 5px, 13px, 22px) create subtle misalignments that accumulate into visual noise.
2. **Faster design decisions** -- Designers and developers choose from 6 values instead of infinite possibilities, reducing bikeshedding.
3. **Predictable responsiveness** -- A base-4 scale divides evenly across common breakpoints and container sizes.
4. **Maintainability** -- Changing the scale in one place propagates cleanly; random values require auditing every usage.

## Assertions

- No CSS property `margin`, `padding`, `gap`, `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, `inset`, or `row-gap`/`column-gap` may contain a value outside the set `[4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32]` (px) or their rem equivalents `[0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2]`.
- Zero (`0`) is always permitted (no spacing).
- Negative margins are allowed only if the absolute value is in the allowed set.
- Token names matching `/space-/` must resolve to one of the allowed values.
